query: Mobile data is now enabled on my phone. What should I do next to try sending MMS messages?

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: Reports trouble sending MMS messages.
assistant: Identified the account and device context, then asked the user to check the phone status bar.
user: Reported that the status bar shows strong signal and 5G, but mobile data is disabled.
assistant: Instructed the user to enable mobile data.
user: Confirmed that mobile data is now enabled and asked what to do next to get MMS working.

candidate_answers.text: Great — with mobile data back on, I want to verify that your line still has usable data service before moving on to app-level MMS checks.
The next grounded backend check is line data usage and limit status, because MMS depends on working mobile data and that can be confirmed before deeper device troubleshooting.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a post-device-toggle state where the most informative remaining backend check is whether the line still has usable mobile data capacity before proceeding to permissions or app-level causes.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. Scores prioritize confirming data availability because the user has just restored data mode and asks what to try next for MMS.
notes: query comes directly from the selected visible user message, not from hidden task text
history_summary stops at the decision point and excludes future assistant or tool outputs
candidate_answers are manual non-leaky drafts grounded only in visible context
This sample captures an intermediate troubleshooting checkpoint distinct from both initial diagnosis and final app-permission repair.
